

# About AWS Elastic Beanstalk release notes
<a name="welcome"></a>

The Elastic Beanstalk release notes provide details about Elastic Beanstalk releases—new features, updates, and fixes related to the service, platform, console, and EB CLI. Whenever we release any update, we publish release notes describing the content of the release.

Elastic Beanstalk has several types of regular releases:
+ *Feature launches*—New functionality or updates to existing features.
+ *Service updates*—Updates to the [Elastic Beanstalk API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/api/).
+ *Platform updates*—Updates to [Elastic Beanstalk platforms](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/concepts-all-platforms.html), which might update existing configurations or add new ones.
+ *Client updates*—Updates to the [Elastic Beanstalk console](https://console.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk) or the [EB CLI](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/eb-cli3.html).

For the list of release notes, see [AWS Elastic Beanstalk release notes](relnotes.md).

For release notes predating this document, which were published elsewhere on the Amazon Web Services website, see [AWS Elastic Beanstalk Release Notes](https://aws.amazon.com/releasenotes/AWS-Elastic-Beanstalk/) .

## Additional resources
<a name="welcome.resources"></a>

The following related resources can help you as you work with this service.
+ **[AWS Elastic Beanstalk Developer Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/)** – Concepts, tutorials, and reference materials.
+ **[AWS Elastic Beanstalk Platforms](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/platforms/)** – Detailed version listings of current and historical configurations of AWS Elastic Beanstalk platforms.
